If the plant you are referring to is Iris versicolor, here is a paragraph about it:

The Iris genus is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Iridaceae. Iris versicolor, a member of this genus, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which may help to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Iris versicolor include iridin, irisorin, and other iridoid glycosides, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Iris versicolor include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
